title: Colombia Faces Uncertain Future as Election Nears Amid Rising Violence
subtitle: As voters prepare to choose a new leader, the country struggles with a return to armed conflict and deep political divisions.

summary.en: Colombia is preparing for a presidential election that is heavily influenced by a sharp rise in political violence. The country remains divided between different approaches to dealing with armed rebel groups. While some leaders support peace deals, others argue for a return to military action. Recent killings and kidnappings have highlighted the failure of previous efforts to end the long-standing conflict. The outcome of this vote will determine how the nation handles its ongoing struggle with criminal factions and drug trafficking.
summary.zh: 哥倫比亞正準備迎接一場深受政治暴力急劇上升影響的總統大選。該國在處理武裝叛亂團體的方針上依然分裂，部分領導人支持和平協議，其他人則主張回歸軍事行動。近期的殺戮與綁架事件凸顯了先前終結長期衝突的努力已告失敗。此次投票結果將決定該國如何應對與犯罪派系及毒品走私之間持續不斷的鬥爭。

paragraphs.en: Colombia is currently preparing for a presidential election that is taking place under a dark cloud of rising political violence. The situation has reached its highest levels in a decade, forcing the country’s long-standing internal armed conflict to the center of the political debate. This election is not just a choice between different candidates; it is a choice between two completely different ways to handle a war that has claimed nearly half a million lives.
paragraphs.zh: 哥倫比亞目前正為總統大選做準備，而選舉是在政治暴力日益猖獗的陰影下進行。局勢已達到十年來的最高點，迫使該國長期以來的內部武裝衝突成為政治辯論的核心。這場選舉不僅是在不同候選人之間做選擇，更是在兩種截然不同的方式之間做抉擇，以處理這場已奪走近五十萬條人命的戰爭。

paragraphs.en: The human cost of this instability is clear. According to The Guardian World, the recent death of 24-year-old journalist Mateo Pérez Rueda has become a symbol of this dangerous environment. Rueda was kidnapped, tortured, and killed by a group known as the 36th Front, which is made up of dissidents—former members who refused to stop fighting—of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc). His death occurred while he was reporting on the ongoing conflict between the army, paramilitaries, and these rebel groups in the province of Antioquia.
paragraphs.zh: 這種不穩定局勢所帶來的人道代價顯而易見。根據《衛報世界版》（The Guardian World）報導，二十四歲記者馬特奧·佩雷斯·魯埃達（Mateo Pérez Rueda）近期遇害，已成為這種危險環境的象徵。魯埃達遭到一個名為「第三十六陣線」的組織綁架、折磨並殺害，該組織由哥倫比亞革命武裝力量（Farc）的異議分子組成，這些前成員拒絕停止戰鬥。他在安蒂奧基亞省（Antioquia）報導軍隊、準軍事組織與這些叛亂團體之間持續不斷的衝突時遇害。

paragraphs.en: This violence is a reminder that while the 2016 peace deal between the government and most of the Farc reduced fighting for several years, it did not end the conflict permanently. The Guardian World reports that some rebel factions refused to sign the agreement, and they have since grown in strength and size. Furthermore, many experts believe that subsequent governments did not move fast enough to put the peace deal into action, allowing these groups to regain power.
paragraphs.zh: 這場暴力事件提醒人們，儘管政府與大多數哥倫比亞革命武裝力量成員在二〇一六年達成的和平協議在幾年內減少了戰鬥，但並未永久終結衝突。《衛報世界版》報導指出，一些叛亂派系拒絕簽署該協議，此後勢力與規模不斷壯大。此外，許多專家認為，後續政府在落實和平協議方面行動不夠迅速，讓這些團體得以重掌權力。

paragraphs.en: The upcoming election features a sharp divide between the left and the right. The current president, Gustavo Petro, cannot run for re-election. Instead, he has given his support to the left-wing senator Iván Cepeda. Cepeda is currently leading in the polls and is the main architect of the government’s “total peace” plan. This plan aims to sign disarmament deals—agreements where groups agree to give up their weapons—with all criminal organizations. While Cepeda remains committed to this path, many security experts argue that the plan has failed. They note that armed groups have used temporary ceasefires—periods where both sides agree to stop fighting—to expand their territory and influence.
paragraphs.zh: 即將到來的選舉呈現出左派與右派之間的尖銳對立。現任總統古斯塔沃·佩特羅（Gustavo Petro）無法競選連任，他轉而支持左派參議員伊凡·塞佩達（Iván Cepeda）。塞佩達目前在民調中領先，也是政府「全面和平」計畫的主要推手。該計畫旨在與所有犯罪組織簽署解除武裝協議，即各團體同意放棄武器的協定。儘管塞佩達仍致力於此途徑，但許多安全專家認為該計畫已經失敗。他們指出，武裝團體利用雙方同意停止戰鬥的臨時停火期，來擴張其領土與影響力。

paragraphs.en: In contrast, the main challengers from the right side of the political spectrum, including lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella and senator Paloma Valencia, have promised a return to all-out war. They argue that the current approach is not working and that a stronger military response is necessary as soon as they take office. This debate over security is happening while the country experiences a surge in guerrilla attacks, kidnappings, and massacres. The Guardian World noted that even political candidates are not safe; last year, the right-wing senator Miguel Uribe Turbay was shot and killed during a campaign event.
paragraphs.zh: 相比之下，來自政治光譜右派的主要挑戰者，包括律師阿貝拉多·德拉埃斯普里耶拉（Abelardo de la Espriella）與參議員帕洛瑪·瓦倫西亞（Paloma Valencia），皆承諾回歸全面戰爭。他們主張目前的做法行不通，且一旦上任，必須採取更強硬的軍事回應。這場關於安全的辯論發生之際，該國正經歷游擊隊攻擊、綁架與屠殺事件的激增。《衛報世界版》指出，連政治候選人都無法倖免；去年，右派參議員米格爾·烏里韋·圖爾拜（Miguel Uribe Turbay）在競選活動中遭槍殺身亡。

paragraphs.en: While the rebel groups often claim to have a political agenda, the reality on the ground is often driven by money. According to Jorge Rueda, a relative of the murdered journalist, the war in areas like Antioquia is largely about control over illegal activities. These include drug production, the smuggling of cocaine, illegal gold mining, and logging. Al Jazeera English reports that in the Catatumbo region, Farc dissidents claim they returned to war because the historic peace deal failed to provide the security and social changes they were promised. These groups are now fighting rivals for control of territory and profitable drug trafficking routes.
paragraphs.zh: 雖然叛亂團體常聲稱擁有政治議程，但實地情況往往是由金錢驅動。根據遇害記者的親屬豪爾赫·魯埃達（Jorge Rueda）所述，安蒂奧基亞等地發生的戰爭，很大程度上是為了控制非法活動，包括毒品生產、古柯鹼走私、非法採金與伐木。半島電視台英語頻道（Al Jazeera English）報導，在卡塔通博（Catatumbo）地區，哥倫比亞革命武裝力量的異議分子聲稱他們重返戰場，是因為歷史性的和平協議未能提供承諾的安全與社會變革。這些團體目前正為了爭奪領土與獲利豐厚的毒品走私路線，與敵對勢力交戰。

paragraphs.en: The scale of the violence remains alarming. On a recent Monday, more than 50 people were killed in clashes between two Farc dissident groups in the southern department of Guaviare. Many of those killed were children and teenagers who had been forced to join these criminal factions. Despite these high numbers, researchers like Alejandro Chala from the Fundación Paz y Reconciliación suggest that the current situation is not yet as bad as it was before the 2016 peace agreement. However, for many Colombians, the feeling that the war never really ended is very real.
paragraphs.zh: 暴力的規模依然令人擔憂。近期的一個週一，在南部的瓜維亞雷省（Guaviare），兩個哥倫比亞革命武裝力量異議團體發生衝突，造成五十多人死亡。許多死者是被迫加入這些犯罪派系的兒童與青少年。儘管傷亡數字高昂，但來自和平與和解基金會（Fundación Paz y Reconciliación）的研究員亞歷杭德羅·查拉（Alejandro Chala）認為，目前的局勢尚未像二〇一六年和平協議簽署前那樣糟糕。然而，對許多哥倫比亞人來說，戰爭從未真正結束的感覺是非常真實的。

paragraphs.en: As the election approaches, the country stands at a crossroads. Voters must decide whether to continue the difficult work of negotiating peace or to return to a strategy of military confrontation. With criminal groups continuing to compete for control over resources and territory, the next president will face the immediate and difficult task of restoring order to a nation that is tired of decades of conflict.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著選舉臨近，該國正處於十字路口。選民必須決定是繼續艱難的和平談判工作，還是回歸軍事對抗的策略。隨著犯罪團體持續爭奪資源與領土控制權，下一任總統將面臨立即且艱鉅的任務，即為一個厭倦了數十年衝突的國家恢復秩序。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A situation that causes worry, sadness, or a feeling that something bad is going to happen.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 陰影、不祥之兆、令人擔憂的氛圍。
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面上是烏雲，這裡用來比喻政治局勢的不安。文中：Colombia is currently preparing for a presidential election that is taking place under a dark cloud of rising political violence.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A person who is responsible for creating or planning a complex system or strategy.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 策劃者、設計者（指計畫的幕後推手）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見指建築師，這裡指計畫的策劃者。文中：Cepeda is currently leading in the polls and is the main architect of the government’s “total peace” plan.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Using all possible effort or resources to achieve something.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 全力以赴的、全面的、徹底的。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡用來形容戰爭規模，指不留餘地的全面開戰。文中：In contrast, the main challengers from the right side of the political spectrum, including lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella and senator Paloma Valencia, have promised a return to all-out war.

keyTerms.definitionEn: The actual situation or reality in a specific place, rather than what is reported or theorized.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 實際情況、現場（常用於 on the ground）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見指地面，這裡指「實際現場」。文中：While the rebel groups often claim to have a political agenda, the reality on the ground is often driven by money.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Colombia prepares to go to polls in election shadowed by resurgence of political violence

sources.name: Al Jazeera English
sources.title: Al Jazeera exclusive interview with rebel FARC faction in Colombia

quiz.question: What is the primary reason given for the recent increase in power of the Farc dissident groups?

options.A: The government officially canceled the 2016 peace agreement.
options.B: The slow implementation of the 2016 peace deal allowed these groups to recover.
options.C: The groups received significant financial support from foreign governments.
options.D: The army stopped monitoring the Antioquia region entirely.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第三段，專家認為後續政府未能迅速執行 2016 年的和平協議，這使得反叛組織有機會重新獲得權力。選項 A 錯誤，因為協議並未被取消，只是執行緩慢。選項 C 文章中並未提及外國資助。選項 D 雖然提到了 Antioquia 地區，但文章並未說軍隊完全停止監控該地。

quiz.question: Based on the article, what can be inferred about the 'total peace' plan proposed by Iván Cepeda?

options.A: It has successfully ended all illegal gold mining in Colombia.
options.B: It is supported by all military leaders and security experts.
options.C: It may be unintentionally helping armed groups strengthen their positions.
options.D: It focuses primarily on increasing the number of soldiers in conflict zones.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第四段，安全專家指出武裝團體利用「臨時停火」期間來擴張地盤與勢力，這暗示了該計畫在執行上出現了反效果。選項 A 文章未提及計畫成功。選項 B 錯誤，因為文中明確提到許多安全專家認為該計畫已失敗。選項 D 錯誤，因為該計畫的核心是「裁軍協議」，而非增加軍隊人數。

quiz.question: In the first paragraph, what does the phrase 'under a dark cloud' imply about the upcoming election?

options.A: The election will be held during a period of bad weather.
options.B: The election is surrounded by an atmosphere of fear and uncertainty.
options.C: The candidates are hiding their true political intentions.
options.D: The election results will likely be kept secret from the public.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。「Under a dark cloud」在此處為比喻用法，意指籠罩在陰影之下，暗示選舉受到政治暴力的威脅，充滿不確定性與憂慮。選項 A 將比喻誤解為字面意義（天氣）。選項 C 與 D 皆與文章脈絡中提到的「政治暴力」與「內部武裝衝突」無關，屬於錯誤的語意推論。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the central theme of the article?

options.A: The life and tragic death of journalist Mateo Pérez Rueda.
options.B: The failure of the Colombian army to defeat the 36th Front.
options.C: The economic benefits of illegal drug trafficking in rural Colombia.
options.D: The difficult choice facing Colombian voters regarding how to address persistent political violence.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。全文的核心在於描述哥倫比亞在選舉前夕面臨的困境：選民必須在「繼續談判和平」與「採取軍事對抗」這兩種截然不同的路線中做出抉擇。選項 A 只是文章用來佐證暴力嚴重的案例。選項 B 與 C 皆為文章中提到的細節，並非整篇文章旨在探討的主題。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
